activation_mode.mechanism: Pi rule files load unconditionally; no conditional or model-decision activation documented

auto_memory.mechanism: Pi does not have an AI-generated auto-memory system

cross_provider_recognition.mechanism: Pi reads its own rule format; no cross-provider rule file recognition documented

file_imports.mechanism: No file import syntax documented for Pi rule files

hierarchical_loading.mechanism: Pi rule files are not documented as supporting multi-level directory loading

providerExtensions.name: Rules delivered via SKILL.md
providerExtensions.description: Pi has no dedicated rules format. Standing instructions are delivered by placing a SKILL.md file in a skill directory and relying on the skills discovery mechanism. Pi reads skills.md as 'instructions' — the same progressive-disclosure system that loads capability skills also loads any SKILL.md that contains standing instructions. Rules can be scoped globally (~/.pi/agent/skills/) or per-project (.pi/skills/) using the same paths as skills.

providerExtensions.name: Cross-harness rules loading via settings array
providerExtensions.description: Because pi's settings skills array accepts paths to other providers' skill directories, rules authored as SKILL.md files for other providers can be loaded directly into a pi session without conversion. This is a side-effect of cross-harness skill loading rather than a dedicated rules feature.
